api.notes: No ollama-ai-provider; ollama is an OpenAI-compatible endpoint. Flag-gated native runtime (experimentalNativeLlm, default off) also uses chat-completions for openai-compatible.

system_prompt.length_chars_note: raw file bytes incl. trailing newline; file is also reproduced verbatim in opencode.md section 2.4
system_prompt.selection_rule: session/system.ts:25-39 SystemPrompt.provider(model) keys on model.api.id substring; ollama names (silver:e4b, gemma4:e4b, etc.) fall through to default.txt. Other branches: gpt-4/o1/o3->beast.txt, gpt+codex->codex.txt, gpt->gpt.txt, gemini- ->gemini.txt, claude->anthropic.txt, trinity->trinity.txt, kimi->kimi.txt
system_prompt.delivery: Single role:'system' message. All parts joined with '\n' at session/llm/request.ts:56-66, then prepended to messages[] at request.ts:101-112. NOT passed via AI SDK system: param. Plugin hook experimental.chat.system.transform may grow to at most 2 system messages (request.ts:74-78); default ollama path = exactly 1.

custom_agent.loading: config/agent.ts:13,26-27 — YAML frontmatter merged into config; trimmed markdown body becomes agent.prompt
custom_agent.replacement_rule: request.ts:60 — when input.agent.prompt is set it REPLACES SystemPrompt.provider(model) (i.e. replaces default.txt). env + instructions + skills still appended after it.
custom_agent.real_example_note: frontmatter tools: map whitelists namespaced names argent_describe, argent_gesture-tap, etc. — confirms namespacing end-to-end

namespacing.sanitize: value.replace(/[^a-zA-Z0-9_-]/g, '_')  -- dashes and underscores PRESERVED, dots/colons/spaces -> _
namespacing.invocation_note: execute calls MCP server with ORIGINAL un-prefixed name: client.callTool({name: mcpTool.name}) (catalog.ts:54-58). Prompts/resources use ':' separator (catalog.ts:103) but are not callable tools.

tool_definition.schema_format: JSON Schema (JSONSchema7), forced type:'object' + additionalProperties:false; properties default {} if absent. For ollama/openai-compatible NOT further sanitized (sanitizeOpenAISchema is openai/azure only).
tool_definition.description_source: MCP server's advertised tool description, verbatim (mcpTool.description ?? '') -- catalog.ts:51
tool_definition.serialized_object_shape: AI SDK dynamicTool({ description, inputSchema: jsonSchema(...), execute }) -> wire { type:'function', function:{ name, description, parameters } }

assistant_tool_call_format.shape: OpenAI tool_calls on assistant message: { role:'assistant', content:'', tool_calls:[{ id, type:'function', function:{ name, arguments } }] }
assistant_tool_call_format.tool_call_id: NOT minted or prefixed by OpenCode for ollama/openai-compatible; model's emitted id used verbatim and echoed in tool result. Claude/Mistral get id scrubbing (transform.ts:190,223-228) but that does not apply here.

tool_result_format.shape: role:'tool' message { role:'tool', tool_call_id, content }. Built as assistant part type:'tool-<name>' state:'output-available' (message-v2.ts:326-334) then convertToModelMessages (message-v2.ts:417) emits the tool role. toModelOutput: string output -> {type:'text', value}; object {text,attachments} -> {type:'content', value:[...]}.
tool_result_format.error_framing: No 'ERROR:' prefix, no isError flag on normal path. Failed tool uses state:'output-error' + errorText (message-v2.ts:348-358). MCP isError -> throws joined text or 'MCP tool returned an error' (catalog.ts:68-73). Placeholders: '[Tool execution was interrupted]' (message-v2.ts:368), '[Old tool result content cleared]' (message-v2.ts:305).

junk.when: Tool returns media AND provider not in supportsMediaInToolResult allowlist (anthropic/openai/bedrock/xai/google-vertex-anthropic/gemini-3). @ai-sdk/openai-compatible (ollama) is NOT in allowlist -> always taken: media re-injected as a separate role:'user' message led by this text + type:'file' parts (message-v2.ts:393-410).

junk.string: ERROR: Cannot read ${name} (this model does not support ${modality} input). Inform the user.
junk.rendered_for_image: ERROR: Cannot read image (this model does not support image input). Inform the user.
junk.rendered_with_filename: ERROR: Cannot read "shot.png" (this model does not support image input). Inform the user.
junk.when: User/synthetic message file/image part whose modality not in model.capabilities.input. Text-only ollama returning a screenshot: image re-injected via 'Attached media...' then REPLACED by this error text -> model sees the error string, not the image.

junk.string: ERROR: Image file is empty or corrupted. Please provide a valid image.
${output}\n\n[${n} image(s) omitted: could not be resized below the image size limit.]
junk.when: Image attachment couldn't be resized under the size cap
Tool output exceeds 2000 lines OR 50KB (unit = 'lines' or 'bytes')

junk.string: The tool call succeeded but the output was truncated. Full output saved to: ${file}\nUse the Task tool to have explore agent process this file with Grep and Read (with offset/limit). Do NOT read the full file yourself - delegate to save context.
junk.when: Truncation hint, when Task tool available

junk.string: The tool call succeeded but the output was truncated. Full output saved to: ${file}\nUse Grep to search the full content or Read with offset/limit to view specific sections.
junk.when: Truncation hint, when Task tool NOT available

junk.string: [Tool output truncated for compaction: omitted ${omitted} chars]

<system-reminder>
The user sent the following message:
${text}

Please address this message and continue with your tasks.
</system-reminder>
junk.when: User interjects mid-run; wraps the injected user text

junk_that_does_NOT_exist: Attached image(s) from tool result: (real string is 'Attached media from tool result:')
tool_result_format.image_handling: Two-step for text-only ollama: (1) media not in supportsMediaInToolResult allowlist -> re-injected as synthetic role:'user' message 'Attached media from tool result:' + file parts (message-v2.ts:158-170, 393-410); (2) unsupportedParts replaces image part with 'ERROR: Cannot read image (this model does not support image input). Inform the user.' if model.capabilities.input.image is false (transform.ts:399-405). If model DOES declare image input, the real image part is sent instead. @ai-sdk/openai-compatible is NOT in the media-in-tool-result allowlist.

truncation.marker: ...${removed} ${unit} truncated... + hint (full output saved to temp file)
truncation.note: argent device-control outputs (describe JSON) are small and rarely hit this; media re-injection is the junk that actually appears in argent trajectories.

content.text: ERROR: Cannot read image (this model does not support image input). Inform the user.

other_junk.as: extra { role:'assistant', content: MAX_STEPS } appended to messages on the final allowed step

other_junk.string: plan-mode reminders from prompt/plan.txt, build-switch.txt, plan-mode.txt (each <system-reminder>-wrapped)

other_junk.note: None of these fire for a normal argent device-control turn except MAX_STEPS on the very last step.

capture_method.approach: Point the ollama provider's options.baseURL at a logging proxy instead of localhost:11434, then run a turn and capture the POST to /v1/chat/completions.
capture_method.config_change: In ~/.config/opencode/opencode.json set provider.ollama.options.baseURL to e.g. http://localhost:8080/v1 (proxy forwards to http://localhost:11434/v1).
capture_method.run: opencode run --model ollama/silver:e4b "Tap the Login button"  (or TUI)

notes: Ollama media gotcha: a tool returning a screenshot does NOT reach a text-only ollama model as an image; it is re-injected as a synthetic user message 'Attached media from tool result:' then the image part is REPLACED by 'ERROR: Cannot read image (this model does not support image input). Inform the user.' Render screenshot-returning tools this way for ollama training data unless model.capabilities.input.image is true.
Exactly ONE role:'system' message, all parts joined with '\n' (default.txt + env + AGENTS + skills); not the SDK system: param.
Namespacing keeps dashes: argent_gesture-tap NOT argent_gesture_tap. Separator '_', tool's own dashes survive sanitize.
No tool-call-ID prefixing by OpenCode for ollama; model's emitted ids used verbatim and echoed in tool results.
default.txt selected because ollama model ids (silver:e4b, gemma4:e4b) lack the gpt/gemini-/claude/trinity/kimi substrings; avoid those substrings in model names to stay on default.txt.
Real user config has both default and a custom 'argentbench' agent; argentbench.md body REPLACES default.txt, env/instructions/skills still appended.
